plan.objective: To investigate the effects of a specific dietary intervention on metabolic parameters in diet-induced obesity mouse models.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will involve two groups of diet-induced obesity mice: one receiving a high-fat diet with a specific dietary intervention and the other receiving a high-fat diet without the intervention. Key metabolic parameters such as body weight, glucose tolerance, and lipid profiles will be measured over a 12-week period.
controls: Mice on a standard chow diet (LabDiet 5001) to compare metabolic outcomes against the high-fat diet groups.
Mice on a high-fat diet without any dietary intervention to assess the baseline effects of obesity.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 week | Animal Preparation
Obtain 20 male C57BL/6J mice (8 weeks old) from Jackson Laboratory and acclimatize them for 1 week in a controlled environment (22°C, 12 h light/dark cycle) with free access to water.
Equipment: Animal housing facility
Expected outcome: Mice acclimatized without stress or health issues.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Diet Induction
Randomly assign mice to two groups (n=10 each): Group A will receive a high-fat diet (60% kcal from fat, Research Diets D12492) and Group B will receive a high-fat diet with a dietary intervention (e.g., 1% curcumin added to the diet).
Equipment: Animal housing facility
Reagents: Research Diets D12492, Curcumin powder (1% w/w)
Expected outcome: Mice are assigned to dietary groups with appropriate diets provided.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Body Weight Measurement
Weigh all mice using an electronic scale (Ohaus Scout Pro SPX123) and record body weights in grams.
Equipment: Ohaus Scout Pro SPX123 electronic scale
Expected outcome: Body weights recorded for all mice.

DAY 7 | 2 h | Glucose Tolerance Test (GTT)
After 6 hours of fasting, administer 1 g/kg body weight of glucose (D-glucose, Sigma-Aldrich) via intraperitoneal injection. Measure blood glucose levels at 0, 30, 60, and 120 minutes using a glucometer (Accu-Chek Aviva).
Equipment: Accu-Chek Aviva glucometer
Reagents: D-glucose (1 g/kg)
Expected outcome: Blood glucose levels measured at specified time points.

DAY 14 | 30 min | Body Weight Measurement
Weigh all mice again using the electronic scale and record body weights in grams.
Equipment: Ohaus Scout Pro SPX123 electronic scale
Expected outcome: Body weights recorded for all mice.

DAY 28 | 2 h | Lipid Profile Analysis
Collect blood samples via tail vein and analyze serum for triglycerides and cholesterol levels using a commercial kit (Cayman Chemical).
Equipment: Cayman Chemical lipid assay kit
Expected outcome: Serum lipid levels quantified.

DAY 84 | 30 min | Final Body Weight Measurement
Weigh all mice for the final time using the electronic scale and record body weights in grams.
Equipment: Ohaus Scout Pro SPX123 electronic scale
Expected outcome: Final body weights recorded for all mice.

DAY 84 | 2 h | Data Analysis
Analyze the data using ANOVA for body weight changes, GTT results, and lipid profiles. Use a significance level of p<0.05 to determine differences between groups.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined for metabolic parameters.

assumptions: The dietary intervention will have a measurable effect on metabolic parameters in the mice.
Mice will respond consistently to the high-fat diet and the dietary intervention over the study period.
The glucose tolerance test will accurately reflect the metabolic state of the mice.
decision_criteria: Proceed if body weight in Group A increases by >10% compared to Group B.
Proceed if glucose levels in Group A are significantly higher than in Group B at 60 min post-injection.
Proceed if lipid levels in Group A are significantly higher than in Group B.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of diets for all groups.
Standardize the time of day for all measurements to minimize variability.
Calibrate the glucometer before each use.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To investigate the effects of a specific dietary intervention on metabolic parameters in diet-induced obesity mouse models.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
